title: Yes Postpones European ‘Fragile’ Tour as Guitarist Steve Howe Prepares for ‘Essential Operation’
summary: "This decision has been made to ensure that Steve can return to the stage in full health," the band wrote.
body: Yes is postponing their European tour so that guitarist Steve Howe can undergo an “essential operation.” The English progressive rock band took to social media on Thursday (April 2) to announce that the group is delaying the 11-date Fragile tour of Europe to allow Howe time to recover from an undisclosed surgery. “The upcoming YES ‘Fragile’ UK and EU Tour, due to commence on 22nd April, has had to be postponed as guitarist Steve Howe requires an essential operation that requires recovery time,” Yes wrote on Instagram . “This decision has been made to ensure that Steve can return to the stage in full health and deliver the performances that fans deserve.” The European leg of the tour was originally scheduled to begin on April 22 at Scotland’s Glasgow Royal Concert Hall and was set to feature a full performance of the band’s 1971 album, Fragile . The tour follows a North American leg, which took place in 2025 . Yes added, “We are working hard to reschedule the UK and EU shows to a later date, with full details to be announced after Easter. Please retain your tickets as they will be valid for the rescheduled dates.” The band…

title: Garmin catches up to Apple and Oura with its latest tracking upgrade
summary: Integration with Natural Cycles brings FDA-cleared insights to users' wrists.
body: Garmin is expanding its women’s health focus. Today, the company announced integration with Natural Cycles, letting users tap their smartwatch data for more advanced fertility tracking and cycle insights. Don’t want to miss the best from Android Authority ?

title: 8 things Google’s Whoop competitor needs before I’ll buy it
summary: A screenless device needs to deliver elsewhere.
body: Google is taking a swing at Whoop with its own screenless Fitbit band, recently teased on the wrists of NBA star Steph Curry. We’ve seen a few similar launches this year from Polar and Amazfit, all of which point to more people looking for a wearable that skips the distractions. The problem is, Whoop already does that well. If Google wants to compete, it needs to deliver on a variety of fronts. 1. Reliable core performance

title: Your Galaxy Watch can finally track your blood pressure — but you probably shouldn’t use it
summary: Estimates aren't the same as readings with a medical device.
body: I used to love drugstore blood pressure machines as a kid. The vague squeeze followed by a string of nonsensical numbers and a smiley face felt as mysterious as it was satisfying. As an adult, I’m much more aware of what those metrics actually mean, and this week, Samsung is bringing them right to your wrist. Blood pressure monitoring is exactly the kind of upgrade I’ve been waiting to see on the Galaxy Watch in the US. Unfortunately, it’s not the tool people who need accurate readings should be using.
